tools.description: Create a new GitHub issue for tracking bugs, feature requests, or tasks. Use this for actionable work items that need assignment, labeling, and status tracking. For reports, announcements, or status updates that don't require task tracking, use create_discussion instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.

body.description: Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

labels.description: Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.

parent.description: Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.

title.description: Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.

tools.name: create_issue

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Dispatch the issue triage workflow for the newly created issue
tools.name: dispatch_triage

tools.description: Get details for a commit from a GitHub repository

include_diff.description: Whether to include file diffs and stats in the response. Default is true.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch name, or tag name

tools.name: get_commit

tools.description: Get the contents of a file or directory from a GitHub repository

ref.description: Accepts optional git refs such as `refs/tags/{tag}`, `refs/heads/{branch}` or `refs/pull/{pr_number}/head`

sha.description: Accepts optional commit SHA. If specified, it will be used instead of ref

tools.name: get_file_contents

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get the latest release in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_latest_release

annotations.title: Get a release by tag name

tools.description: Get a specific release by its tag name in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_release_by_tag

tools.description: Get details about a specific git tag in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_tag

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List branches in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_branches

tools.description: Get list of commits of a branch in a GitHub repository. Returns at least 30 results per page by default, but can return more if specified using the perPage parameter (up to 100).

author.description: Author username or email address to filter commits by

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch or tag name to list commits of. If not provided, uses the default branch of the repository. If a commit SHA is provided, will list commits up to that SHA.

tools.name: list_commits

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: List releases in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_releases

tools.description: List git tags in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_tags

tools.description: Fast and precise code search across ALL GitHub repositories using GitHub's native search engine. Best for finding exact symbols, functions, classes, or specific code patterns.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub's powerful code search syntax. Examples: 'content:Skill language:Java org:github', 'NOT is:archived language:Python OR language:go', 'repo:github/github-mcp-server'. Supports exact matching, language filters, path filters, and more.

tools.name: search_code

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

tools.description: Find GitHub repositories by name, description, readme, topics, or other metadata. Perfect for discovering projects, finding examples, or locating specific repositories across GitHub.

minimal_output.description: Return minimal repository information (default: true). When false, returns full GitHub API repository objects.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Repository search query. Examples: 'machine learning in:name stars:>1000 language:python', 'topic:react', 'user:facebook'. Supports advanced search syntax for precise filtering.

sort.description: Sort repositories by field, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_repositories

resource.text:
# Release History

## 1.0.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)

### Features Added

- Initial release of Azure.GeneratorAgent, a powerful CLI tool for automated Azure SDK code generation and migration workflows
- Added `generate` command for automated Azure SDK code generation with validation and git integration
- Added `migrate` command for migrating existing code to new Azure SDK patterns
- SDK path validation with comprehensive error handling and logging
- Git repository integration for commit tracking and validation
- Configurable logging levels and workflow settings through appsettings.json
- Cross-platform support with proper cancellation token handling

### Breaking Changes

- Initial release, no breaking changes

### Bugs Fixed

- Initial release, no bugs fixed

### Other Changes

- Initial implementation with extensible architecture for future SDK workflow automation

# Azure Generator Agent client library for .NET

An MCP (Model Context Protocol) server that exposes deterministic fix tools for automating Azure SDK code generation and migration workflows. A skill (LLM agent) calls these tools directly to handle rule-based fixes, keeping LLM usage for only non-deterministic reasoning.

## Getting started

### Prerequisites

- [.NET 10.0](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download/dotnet/10.0) or later
- Git (for repository operations)

### Build and run

Build from source within the azure-sdk-for-net repository:

```bash
cd sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent
dotnet build
```

## Key concepts

The Azure Generator Agent automates SDK code generation workflows, including code generation from service specifications and migration to new SDK patterns.

### Architecture

The agent uses a three-layer architecture:

- **MCP Server** — Exposes deterministic fix tools over the [Model Context Protocol](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/) via stdio transport. Tools are auto-discovered from the assembly at startup.
- **MCP Tools** — 18 individual tools covering regex replacements (field renames, type patterns), adding/removing using directives, nullable annotation fixes, build output parsing, error classification, code generation, test execution, commit iteration, and finalization. Each tool supports both MCP (JSON) and in-process invocation.
- **Skill-Driven Workflow** — The skill doc ([`sdk-migration`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/.github/skills/sdk-migration/SKILL.md)) IS the orchestrator. The LLM reads it, calls MCP tools directly, and reasons about what to do next. No compiled C# orchestrator — the skill drives the build→classify→fix→rebuild loop.

### MCP Tools

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `regex_replacement` | Perform regex-based find/replace in source files (field renames, type patterns, namespace fixes) |
| `add_using_directive` | Add a using directive to a C# file if not already present |
| `remove_using_directive` | Remove using directives matching a namespace pattern |
| `nullable_annotation_fix` | Fix CS8625/CS8600 by adding `?` nullable annotation on a specific line |
| `batch_fix` | Apply multiple deterministic fixes in a single call |
| `build_and_classify` | Run `dotnet build`, parse output, and classify each error as deterministic or requires-reasoning |
| `classify_errors` | Classify a batch of build errors against the deterministic fix registry |
| `run_code_generation` | Run `dotnet build /t:generateCode` for a project. Accepts optional `localSpecsPath` for local spec iteration. |
| `validate_tsp_config` | Validate that `tspconfig.yaml` has the correct emitter configuration |
| `commit_iteration` | Iterate through spec repo commits to find one with valid tspconfig. Accepts optional `commitOverride` to skip iteration. |
| `pregen_cleanup` | Remove `IncludeAutorestDependency` from `.csproj` files before first generation |
| `migrate_test_samples` | Move test samples from `Generated/Samples/` to `Samples/` |
| `finalize_migration` | Run `Export-API.ps1` and `Update-Snippets.ps1` after a successful migration |
| `run_tests` | Run `dotnet test` with configurable filter (defaults to excluding live tests). Returns structured pass/fail results. |
| `rename_codegen_type` | Fix mismatched `[CodeGenType]` attributes by matching generated counterparts |
| `fetch_to_fromlro` | Replace legacy `Fetch(response)` calls with `ResponseModel.FromLroResponse(response)` |

### Deterministic Fix Registry

The `DeterministicFixRegistry` contains rules that map error codes and message patterns to specific tool invocations. Rules cover:

- **Field renames** (9 mappings) — `_pipeline` → `Pipeline`, `_clientDiagnostics` → `ClientDiagnostics`, `_endpoint` → `Endpoint`, `_serializedAdditionalRawData` → `_additionalBinaryDataProperties`, etc.
- **Type pattern replacements** — `ResponseWithHeaders<T,H>` → `Response<T>`, `Rest.TypeName` → `TypeName`, `Models.Models.X` → `Models.X`, `CodeGenModel` → `CodeGenType`
- **Missing using directives** — 47 type-to-namespace mappings across `Azure.Core`, `Azure.Core.Pipeline`, `Azure`, `System.ClientModel`, `System.ClientModel.Primitives`, `Azure.ResourceManager`, and `Microsoft.TypeSpec.Generator.Customizations`
- **Obsolete using removal** — `Autorest.*`, `.Rest` namespaces
- **Nullable annotations** — CS8625/CS8600 fixes
- **Method call replacements** — `FromCancellationToken` → `ToRequestContext`, `Fetch(response)` → `FromLroResponse`, obsolete `.ToRequestContent()` removal

See [`.github/skills/sdk-migration/SKILL.md`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/.github/skills/sdk-migration/SKILL.md) for the full rule list, tool usage guide, and the skill-driven workflow.

## Examples

### Use with a migration skill

The tools are designed to be called by the [`sdk-migration`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/.github/skills/sdk-migration/SKILL.md) skill. The typical workflow is:

1. **Setup** — `pregen_cleanup` → `validate_tsp_config` → `commit_iteration` → `run_code_generation`
2. **Build-fix loop** — `build_and_classify` → `batch_fix` (for deterministic errors) → LLM reasoning (for non-deterministic errors) → rebuild
3. **Finalize** — `migrate_test_samples` → `run_tests` → `finalize_migration`

### Use with Copilot Chat

To trigger an MCP-assisted migration in Copilot Chat, use a prompt like:

```
Use the sdk-migration skill to migrate <repo-root>/sdk/<service>/<library>
The local specs repo is at <specs-root>/specification/<service>/<spec-directory>
```

For example:

```
Use the sdk-migration skill to migrate C:\git\azure-sdk-for-net\sdk\communication\Azure.Communication.Messages
The local specs repo is at C:\git\azure-rest-api-specs\specification\communication\Communication.Messages
```
If you wish you use specific commit id for tsp-location.yaml add to the message. For example:

```
Use the sdk-migration skill to migrate C:\git\azure-sdk-for-net\sdk\communication\Azure.Communication.Messages
The local specs repo is at C:\git\azure-rest-api-specs\specification\communication\Communication.Messages with commit id xyz
```

The skill will orchestrate the full migration by calling MCP tools automatically.

## Troubleshooting

### Common issues

- **Generation failures**: Check that all prerequisites are installed and the target directory has proper permissions
- **Authentication errors**: Ensure you're running within a properly configured Azure SDK repository
- **Missing dependencies**: Verify .NET 10.0 runtime is installed
- **ModelContextProtocol package not found**: The NuGet.Config must include a source mapping for the `ModelContextProtocol` package to nuget.org

## Contributing

This project welcomes contributions and suggestions. Most contributions require you to agree to a Contributor License Agreement (CLA) declaring that you have the right to, and actually do, grant us the rights to use your contribution.

### Adding new deterministic fix rules

1. Open `src/Mcp/DeterministicFixRegistry.cs`
2. Add a new `FixRule` to the `BuildRules()` method
3. Add a corresponding test in `tests/DeterministicFixRegistryTests.cs`
4. For new type→namespace mappings, add to the `TypeToNamespace` dictionary

## Next steps

- Explore the [Azure SDK for .NET repository](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net)
- Learn about [Azure SDK design guidelines](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/)
- Read the [SDK migration skill](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/.github/skills/sdk-migration/SKILL.md) for the full migration workflow

arguments.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.GeneratorAgent` **Service directory:** `sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/` **Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (#57912) by @radhgupta ## What Changed PR #57912 force-tracked `TestResult.cs` which was previously ignored by a `.gitignore` pattern. This file defines a new public `TestResult` class — the structured return type of the `run_tests` MCP tool — with properties for `Success`, `ExitCode`, `Passed`, `Failed`, `Skipped`, `Total`, `Failures`, `Error`, and `RawOutput`. ## Gaps Found - `TestResult` is a public API type with 9 properties but is not mentioned anywhere in `README.md` or `CHANGELOG.md` - Three MCP tool implementations exist on disk but are entirely absent from the README's MCP Tools table: `AddCodeGenSuppressTool` (`add_codegen_suppress`), `DiscoverProjectTool` (`discover_project`), and `GeneratedSnapshotTool` (`generated_snapshot`) - The README text states "18 individual tools" but the table lists only 16 — the count is now inconsistent with both the table and the actual implementation - The `CHANGELOG.md` for `1.0.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` does not mention the `TestResult` structured result type as a feature addition
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File 1: `sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/README.md`** — Edit In the **MCP Tools** section, replace the table (currently 16 rows) with an updated table that includes the 3 missing tools and fixes the count: 1. Update the introductory sentence from "18 individual tools" to "19 individual tools" (or the actual count after auditing all `*Tool.cs` files). 2. Add the following rows to the MCP Tools table (insert in alphabetical order by tool name): ```markdown | `add_codegen_suppress` | Add a `[CodeGenSuppress]` attribute to a type member in a customization file | | `discover_project` | Discover the SDK project path and metadata for a given service directory | | `generated_snapshot` | Take a snapshot of the Generated/ folder contents for before/after comparison | ``` 3. After the MCP Tools table, add a new subsection documenting the structured result types: ```markdown ### Result Types The following types are returned by MCP tools and can be inspected by the calling skill: #### `TestResult` Returned by `run_tests`. Contains a structured summary of a `dotnet test` run: | Property | Type | Description | |----------|------|-------------| | `Success` | `bool` | `true` if all tests passed (exit code 0) | | `ExitCode` | `int` | Raw process exit code from `dotnet test` | | `Passed` | `int` | Number of tests that passed | | `Failed` | `int` | Number of tests that failed | | `Skipped` | `int` | Number of tests that were skipped | | `Total` | `int` | Total number of tests executed | | `Failures` | `List` | Human-readable descriptions of each failed test | | `Error` | `string?` | Top-level error message if the run itself failed (e.g., build error before tests ran) | | `RawOutput` | `string` | Full raw stdout/stderr from the `dotnet test` process | ``` **File 2: `sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/CHANGELOG.md`** — Edit Under `## 1.0.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` → `### Features Added`, append: ```markdown - Added `TestResult` structured type returned by the `run_tests` MCP tool, exposing `Success`, `ExitCode`, `Passed`, `Failed`, `Skipped`, `Total`, `Failures`, `Error`, and `RawOutput` properties for programmatic inspection of test run outcomes - Added `add_codegen_suppress` tool for inserting `[CodeGenSuppress]` attributes into customization files - Added `discover_project` tool for locating SDK project metadata from a service directory path - Added `generated_snapshot` tool for capturing before/after snapshots of the `Generated/` folder ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new snippet placeholders are required for this change. The `TestResult` type documentation can be added as a plain Markdown table in the README (not a code snippet extracted from a test file), since it is reference documentation rather than executable usage. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/README.md` already contains all six required sections in order: 1. ✅ Getting started (install, prerequisites, build) 2. ✅ Key concepts (architecture, MCP tools table, fix registry) 3. ✅ Examples (migration skill, Copilot Chat usage) 4. ✅ Troubleshooting (common issues) 5. ✅ Contributing (adding new fix rules) 6. ✅ Next steps (external links) The only changes needed are within the existing **Key concepts → MCP Tools** subsection (add missing tool rows and fix count) and adding a new **Result Types** subsection under **Key concepts**. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/src/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/tests/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 tools` — regenerates the public API listing; `TestResult` being public means the API surface changed 4. `dotnet format sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/src/` No snippet update is needed (`Update-Snippets.ps1` is not required here).
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
arguments.title: [GeneratorAgent] Docs: Document TestResult type and missing MCP tools in README
